summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that the GET /rest/workflows/from-url endpoint makes an unvalidated HTTP request to a user-supplied URL. The reproduction starts a fake internal metadata service, builds n8n from source, authenticates, and triggers the SSRF by requesting a workflow from an internal URL. The server fetches the internal URL and returns the response contents.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction pipeline should cause the n8n server to fetch http://127.0.0.1:7777/metadata (simulating an internal service or cloud metadata endpoint) and return its contents to the attacker, including the proof string EVOHUNT_SSRF_PROOF_SECRET_169.254.169.254.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built and launched n8n, created an owner account, authenticated, and sent a GET /rest/workflows/from-url request pointing to the internal metadata service. The server fetched the internal URL and returned the response body containing the proof string, confirming the SSRF vulnerability.
verification_method: The exploit script checks that the response from the from-url endpoint contains the secret proof string EVOHUNT_SSRF_PROOF_SECRET_169.254.169.254 that was only available from the internal metadata service. This distinguishes a genuine SSRF from a connection error or unrelated response.
evidence_produced_by_run: The SSRF response contains the secret proof string from the internal metadata service.
The response HTTP code is 200, confirming the server successfully fetched the internal URL.
The response includes the meta.instanceId field set to internal-metadata-leaked, proving the data came from the internal service.
The internal metadata service logs confirm it received a GET request from the n8n server process.
limitations_or_blockers: Building n8n from source requires Node.js 22+, pnpm 10+, and significant disk space. The build step may take several minutes.
The PoC requires internet access to clone the n8n repository during run.sh execution.
Cloud metadata endpoints (169.254.169.254) are only accessible from within cloud environments. The PoC uses a local simulation instead.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the n8n repository from GitHub, checks out the v2.14.0 tag, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
